title: Reverse Linked List
description:
Given the `head` of a singly linked list, reverse the list and return the new head.

**Define a `ListNode` class** in your solution with `val` and `next` (default `None`), matching the usual interview form. The judge passes the list as a Python list of values `[v1, v2, ...]` and compares your result to the reversed value list.
constraints: The number of nodes is in the range [0, 5000]

hint_plan.level_1: Think about reversing pointers one node at a time.
hint_plan.level_2: Iterative: track previous, current, next; flip current.next to previous.
hint_plan.level_3: Initialize prev=None, cur=head; while cur: nxt=cur.next; cur.next=prev; prev=cur; cur=nxt; return prev.
hint_plan.level_4: Handle empty head early; ensure you return the old tail as new head.

canonical_solution_summary: Iterative pointer reversal or recursive version with careful stack depth.
